After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After pressing LEFT:
- Left feet: moved from cols 14-18 to cols 9-13 (left 5 cols) - they were NOT blocked!
- Right feet: moved from cols 44-48 to cols 49-53 (right 5 cols)
So both feet moved. The left feet moved left from 14-18 to 9-13, and the right feet moved right from 44-48 to 49-53. This confirms that LEFT moves left feet left and right feet right, and both can move simultaneously.
So the horizontal movement is:
- LEFT: left feet left, right feet right
- RIGHT: left feet right, right feet left
And the feet are constrained by the body shape. The left feet can move between cols 9-23 (within the left body), and the right feet can move between cols 39-53 (within the right body).
Now the feet are at:
- Left: rows 39-43, cols 9-13
- Right: rows 39-43, cols 49-53
The right feet are at cols 49-53, which is the "hand" area. The left feet are at cols 9-13, which is the left edge of the body.
I need to get the left feet to cols 14-18 (the "hand" area) while keeping the right feet at cols 49-53. But pressing RIGHT would move the left feet right (good) but also move the right feet left (bad).
Wait, but earlier when the left feet were at cols 9-13 and I pressed RIGHT, the left feet moved to cols 14-18, and the right feet stayed at cols 44-48 (blocked). So the right feet were blocked from moving left.
Now the right feet are at cols 49-53. If I press RIGHT, the right feet would try to move left, but they might be blocked at cols 49-53 (the right edge of the body). Let me test this.
Actually, looking at the image, the right feet are at cols 49-53, which is the right edge of the right body. If I press RIGHT, the right feet would try to move left, but they might be blocked. Let me test this.
But wait, I need to think about this more carefully. The right feet are at cols 49-53, and the right body extends from cols 39-53. So the right feet are at the right edge of the right body. If I press RIGHT, the right feet would try to move left (to cols 44-48), but they might not be blocked since cols 44-48 is within the body.
Actually, earlier when the right feet were at cols 44-48 and I pressed RIGHT, they stayed at cols 44-48. This suggests that the right feet are blocked from moving left when they're at cols 44-48. But now they're at cols 49-53, and pressing RIGHT would try to move them left to cols 44-48. Would they be blocked?
Let me test this by pressing RIGHT once.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current feet: rows 39-43, cols 9-13 (left) and rows 39-43, cols 49-53 (right)
# Press RIGHT: left feet move right (9-13 -> 14-18), right feet move left (49-53 -> ?)
# Earlier, right feet at 44-48 were blocked from moving left when pressing RIGHT
# Now right feet at 49-53, pressing RIGHT would try to move them left to 44-48
# Let's test

action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in node['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in node['boundary']]
        print(f"Feet: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Feet: rows 39-43, cols 14-18
Feet: rows 39-43, cols 44-48
